report.title: Regulatory & Compliance Framework: Mapping Global Standards to the Enterprise AI Stack
report.abstract: This report provides a granular mapping of the EU AI Act, NIS2, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001 to the enterprise AI stack. It details specific technical controls for RAG provenance, autonomous agent risk management, and zero-trust data protection to ensure regulator-defensible compliance for Fortune 500 and G-SIFI organizations.
report.content:
# 1. Regulatory Mapping Matrix (2026–2030)
## 1.1 EU AI Act Enforcement (High-Risk & GPAI) The enterprise stack must automate conformity assessments for systems crossing the $10^{26}$ FLOP threshold or classified as High-Risk (Annex III). - **Article 11 (Technical Documentation)**: Handled by the **Immune System** (Sentinel v2.4). Automated generation of Annex IV dossiers from Kafka-streamed telemetry. - **Article 14 (Human Oversight)**: Enforced at the **Experience Layer**. Mandatory "Stop-Work" UI gates for A-HITL verification. - **Article 15 (Accuracy, Robustness, Cybersecurity)**: Verified in the **Foundation Layer** via HardwareEnclaveAttestor and adversarial red-teaming in CI/CD.
## 1.2 NIS2 & NIST AI RMF Integration - **NIS2 Compliance**: mTLS-secured gRPC communication (EAIP) ensures supply chain security and encrypted reasoning paths. - **NIST AI RMF 2.0**: Operationalized via the **Immune System**. "Map, Measure, Manage" functions are visualized in the CAIO dashboard using OPA-Rego scores.
---
# 2. RAG Provenance & Hardening Controls
## 2.1 Context Provenance (Memory Layer) For RAG systems, provenance is critical for fiduciary and regulatory defensibility (GDPR/FCRA). - **Provenance Chains**: Every retrieved fragment is tagged with a cryptographic hash in the **Recursive Context Envelope (RCE)**. - **Zero-Trust Retrieval**: PII-redaction middleware intercepts the retrieval path, scrubbing data before the **Brain Layer** processes the context.
## 2.2 Hardening the Memory Layer - **Prompt Injection Mitigation**: Semantic filtering at the **Nervous System** layer detects malicious intent in inbound reasoning streams. - **Vector DB RBAC**: Granular access control at the embedding level, integrated with AWS IAM/Azure AD.
---
# 3. Autonomous Agent Risk Management
## 3.1 Agentic Homeostasis & Alignment Autonomous agents are managed via a "Tiered Constraint" model: - **Tier 1 (Critical)**: Agents with transaction authority (e.g., AGI-TRADER). Mandatory air-gapped execution and real-time mechanistic interpretability. - **Tier 2 (Standard)**: Corporate agents (e.g., HR/Marketing). OPA-based policy enforcement and 24-hour log review.
## 3.2 Escalation & Tripwires - **Semantic Tripwires**: If an agent attempts to access restricted namespaces (e.g., `/secrets/vault`), the **Immune System** triggers a SEV-1 pause. - **Recursive Resource Guard**: Integer TTLs in EAIP headers prevent infinite reasoning loops and computational denial-of-service.
---
# 4. ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS Implementation
## 4.1 Continuous Compliance Engine (CCE) The CCE (Python Serverless) asynchronously audits the **Immune System** logs against the ISO 42001 control matrix. - **Control A.7.2 (AI System Lifecycle)**: Automated capture of model versioning, training data hashes, and alignment scores. - **Control A.9.1 (Impact Assessment)**: Real-time bias monitoring at the **Experience Layer**, triggering Jira tickets for remediation if delta exceeds +/- 5%.
